title: (H4) lifting of base automorphism through the Galois cover is assumed, not established in broad generality

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The main structural theorem (Theorem `thm:main`) is stated *conditionally on (H4)*: every variety automorphism $\bar\varphi$ of $X/\Gamma$ induced (via Lemma `lem:centroid`) by an automorphism of $M^\Gamma$ must lift to a $k$-variety automorphism $\varphi$ of $X$ normalizing the $\Gamma$-action. The proof of `lem:varietypart` itself states this is NOT automatic: there is a genuine obstruction, a class in $H^1(X/\Gamma,\underline{\mathrm{Aut}}\ \Gamma\text{-cover})$.

Since the problem asks for the automorphism group *in broad generality*, leaving (H4) as an unverified hypothesis is a real gap.

- Loop/multiloop: the lift is only sketched ('cover is a power map, $\bar\varphi$ monomial'). One must (i) exhibit the lift explicitly, e.g. a rescaling $s\mapsto bs$ on $X/\Gamma=\mathbb G_m$ lifts to $t\mapsto b^{1/m}t$ via a choice of $m$-th root; (ii) verify it normalizes $\Gamma$; (iii) show ALL of $\mathrm{Aut}(\mathbb G_m)=k^\times\rtimes\mathbb Z/2$ (resp. $(k^\times)^n\rtimes\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb Z)$ preserving $\mu$) is realized.
- The general claim '(H4) holds whenever $\mathrm{Pic}(X/\Gamma)$ and $H^1_{\text{ét}}(X/\Gamma,\Gamma)$ vanish (e.g. $X/\Gamma$ a torus), by Pianzola' is cited, not proved, and the precise cohomology controlling the lift is not stated.

Suggested fix: state and prove the explicit monomial lift for tori; isolate (H4) with a precise citation (Gille--Pianzola/Pianzola $H^1$-vanishing) for the general case.
